steps.title: Fastest check: compare a table or graph
steps.content: For an equivalent-expression question, enter the original expression and the answer choices in a calculator table or graph. The correct choice must match for several inputs, not just one. The matching choice is C, $6\left(5x^2+x+1\right)$.

steps.title: Algebra check
steps.content: Use the work below to confirm the identity for every allowed input. This matters because one random matching input is only a quick screen, not a proof that two expressions are always equivalent.

steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Find the choice that equals $30x^{2}+6x+6$ by factoring. The GCF of 30, 6, and 6 is 6, giving $6(5x^{2}+x+1)$.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $5x^2(6x+6)$: expands to $30x^{3}+30x^{2}$, not $30x^{2}+6x+6$. ✗
  • Choice B — $30x(x+1)$: expands to $30x^{2}+30x$, not $30x^{2}+6x+6$. ✗
  • Choice C — $6\left(5x^2+x+1\right)$: expands to $30x^{2}+6x+6$. ✓
  • Choice D — $4\left(2x^2+x+1\right)$: expands to $8x^{2}+4x+4$, not $30x^{2}+6x+6$. ✗
That is the correct answer.
